titleStmt.title: SII 26.721: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 721. (A.R. No. 670 of 1909.) TIRUNEḌUṄGAḶAM, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription refers to the king as ‘who was pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam (i.e., Ceylon), Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records the gift of the land to the north of Tyāgavallich-chatupēdimaṅgalam, to the east of Vikkiramābaraṇanallūr, to the south of [.]ttarambūr and to the south and west of Māṅgāḷal, and all the incomes derived from the village to the temple of Tiruneḍuṅgaḷam-uḍaiya-nāyanār in Vaḍakavira-nāḍu in Pāṇḍikulāśaṉi-vaḷanāḍu, by Vaḷappakkuḍaiyāṉ Tillai Tirunaṭṭapperumāḷ, alias Viśaiyālaiya Muttaraiyaṉ of Vaḷambakkuḍi. It is stated that the donor gave the land to the Śībhaṇḍāram of the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīmaturaiyum Īḻamum karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭaruḷi
ya śrītiripuvaṉa viratevaṟku yāṇṭu muppattiraṇṭāvatu pāṇṭikulācaṉi vaḷanāṭṭu vaṭakaviranāṭṭu Uṭaiyār tiruneṭu
ṅkaḷamuṭaiya nāyanārkku vaḷampakuṭi vaḷappakuṭaiyāṉ tillai tirunaṭṭap perumāḷāṉa vicaiyālaiya muttarayaṉen In
nāyaṉār tevatāṉaṅ kāṭaruppaṟṟu veṉṉukkumeṟām tiyākavalliccatuppetimaṅkalattu Ellaikku vaṭakkum vikkira
māparaṇanallūr Ellaikkuk kiḻakkum ttarampūrellaikku teṟkum māṅkāḷal Ellaikkut teṟkum meṟkum naṭuvupaṭṭa viḷai
nilam puncai mānila nirnilai kāṭu karampai Uṭpaṭa Antarāyam cilvari puṟaveṭṭi Uḷḷiṭṭa Epperpaṭṭa Aracāyaṅkaḷum
taruvellai taḷḷukkaṭaiyiṟai ṟai taṭṭārppāṭṭam Īḻam puncey kūḷavari Uḷḷiṭṭa Āyaṅkaḷum Enakkum Envaṅkicat
ttārkkum naṉṟāka Itu nāyanārkke cipaṇṭāramākak kuṭuttu Āticaṇṭecuratevar cipātattile nirvāṟtuk
ṭutteṉ veḷampakuṭi vaḷappakuṭaiyān tillaitirunaṭṭapperumāḷāṉa vicaiyālaiya muttarayaṉen Ippaṭi
tarāyaccella nakuṭiyil Uḷḷārum (ma)maṟṟum Ittanmam rakṣippār cipātaṅkaḷentalaimeleṉa § Ippaṭi
kku Ivai vicaiyālaiya muttarayan Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 26.721 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
